playlists.name: Golden Ear: The Audiophile Archive
playlists.description: Curated for the 'Velvet' signature of the iFi ZEN DAC V2 via Ropieee/DLNA. This archive serves as a critical benchmark for holographic vocal imaging, complex Big Band transients, and high-resolution orchestral decay. Each track is selected to evaluate bit-perfect transparency from source to transducer.

songs.title: 500 Miles
songs.notes: The 'Original' Asian Folk reference. Unlike Yao Si Ting's airy version, Felicia's is more grounded and woody. Tests for 'Mid-Range Density'. The iFi Burr-Brown chip renders the acoustic guitar resonance with a beautiful vintage weight. Target: 2021 EMI/Universal Remaster.

songs.title: Sealed With A Kiss
songs.notes: The 'Vocal Intimacy' benchmark. Features a very close-mic production. In your MU-X, her voice should feel like it's hovering right in front of your face. Use it to check for 'Holographic Imaging'—you should hear the slight reverb tail reflecting off the studio walls.

songs.title: Let It Be Me
songs.notes: Reference for 'Vocal Balance'. A delicate ballad that tests the crossover between your mid-bass and tweeters. On a high-res master, the transition from her lower register to her head voice should be seamless, without any 'thinning out' at the crossover point.

songs.title: Monday Morning
songs.notes: The 'Transient Speed' test. Features rhythmic acoustic strumming. The 'Velvet' signature of the iFi DAC tames the brightness of the strings while keeping the 'snap' of the fingers. Great for testing the 'Timing' of your system.

songs.title: The Rose
songs.notes: The 'Vocal Projection' test. This track is very intimate. In your MU-X, the voice should feel like it is coming from exactly 12 inches above the center of the dashboard. Use it to check for 'Midrange Dryness'—her voice should sound 'wet' and lush, not like paper.

songs.title: A Lover's Concerto
songs.notes: Reference for 'Instrumental Layering'. Features a melodic orchestral backing. The iFi DAC should separate the violins from the woodwinds with surgical precision. If they sound 'blended,' your DSP crossover is likely too high.

songs.title: A New Day Has Come
songs.notes: The 'Dynamic Build' benchmark. A cover of the Celine Dion classic, but much more delicate. Tests your system's 'Headroom'—it moves from near-silence to a swelling chorus. Perfect for your Java validator's 'RMS Range' calculation.

songs.title: Que Sera, Sera
songs.notes: The 'High-Frequency Air' test. Focuses on the extreme clarity of her voice. The 'S' and 'Sh' sounds here will reveal if your car's tweeters are too bright. On a high-res (24/96) master, the 'Air' around the notes should feel physically present.

songs.title: 500 Miles
songs.notes: The Jazz-Vocal Benchmark. Tests for midrange 'texture'. Her breathing and the 'wetness' of the vocal recording should be clearly audible. Evaluates the soundstage depth and instrument separation of the iFi ZEN DAC. The silence between notes should be 'Jet Black'.

songs.title: 500 Miles
songs.notes: The 'Atmospheric' Folk Reference. Recorded live at the Geneva Opera House. Provides a massive soundstage that tests for 'Room Ambience'. You should be able to hear the physical distance between the three microphones. Target: 24-bit/192kHz Warner Master.

songs.title: 500 Miles
songs.notes: The 'Vocal Microscope' test. Yao Si Ting is known for a very high-pitched, crystal-clear delivery. This recording features an extremely low noise floor. Tests for 'Sibilance Control' and 'Micro-Detail'—you should hear every lip movement and breath. Target: 24-bit/96kHz DSD or MQA-CD master.

songs.title: Leaving on a Jet Plane
songs.notes: The direct successor to '500 Miles'. Features beautiful three-part harmony. Tests for 'Sibilance Control' on your iFi DAC—the 'S' sounds should be soft. If they whistle, your 8kHz DSP gain is too high.

songs.title: Four Strong Winds
songs.notes: The Canadian equivalent of '500 Miles'. Features a very 'woody' acoustic guitar and intimate dual vocals. The iFi Burr-Brown chip renders the 'vocal texture' with immense realism. Target: 24-bit/96kHz Vanguard remaster.

songs.title: Early Morning Rain
songs.notes: The 'Soundstage Depth' benchmark. Features a lonely, traveling vibe similar to '500 Miles'. In your MU-X, the guitar should feel like it's coming from the hood of the car, while the vocals stay pinned to the rearview mirror.

songs.title: The Boxer
songs.notes: The 'Layering' test. Starts simple but adds huge orchestral elements. Tests your system's 'Dynamic Headroom'—on the iFi setup, the drums at the end should be explosive but controlled.

songs.title: Fragile
songs.notes: The benchmark for 'Acoustic Decay'. Features Sting’s delicate nylon-string guitar work. Use this to test for 'Air'—the space around the guitar and vocals should feel 3D. The iFi Burr-Brown chip will prevent the nylon strings from sounding 'plastic' or digital.

songs.title: Shape of My Heart
songs.notes: Reference for 'Guitar Transient Speed'. Dominic Miller's iconic riff requires high phase-coherence to hear the subtle finger-slides. Avoid modern loud remasters; the original 1993 CD (DR14) is superior for the iFi's Velvet signature.

songs.title: Fields of Gold
songs.notes: Elite test for 'Soundstage Width'. Features a lush, layered production with delicate synth-pads and a warm, centered vocal. In your MU-X, the synths should bloom from the A-pillars, while Sting's voice stays locked in the center.

songs.title: Why Should I Cry for You?
songs.notes: The ultimate 'QSound' spatial test. This album was mixed with QSound technology to create a virtual surround effect on two speakers. If your DSP and time alignment are correct, the percussion should sound like it is coming from *beside* your ears.

songs.title: 25 Minutes
songs.notes: The benchmark for 'Center-Stage Vocal Focus'. Features a dry, uncompressed vocal production. The snare drum has a specific 'wooden' snap that tests for mid-bass timing. Avoid modern loud remasters; the 1993 original has a superior DR12+ rating.

songs.title: That's Why (You Go Away)
songs.notes: Reference for 'Piano Resonance' and vocal layering. The acoustic guitar in the intro should sound metallic and physical. The iFi Velvet signature prevents the high-tenor harmonies from sounding 'thin' or 'digital'.

songs.title: Paint My Love
songs.notes: The ultimate 'String Lushness' test. Features a wide orchestral backing. Perfect for evaluating 'Soundstage Width' in your Isuzu MU-X—the strings should bloom from the A-pillars, wrapping around the cabin while the piano stays dead-center.

songs.title: Take Me To Your Heart
songs.notes: Modern Pop-Rock reference. A cover of Jacky Cheung's 'Goodbye Kiss'. Features cleaner, more digital production. Use this to test for 'Sibilance' control—if the 'S' sounds are sharp, your tweeter phase might be out by 180 degrees.

songs.title: ...Baby One More Time
songs.notes: Produced by Max Martin. Features a massive, 'heavy' snare drum and deep synth bass. The 24-bit/44.1kHz remaster (released for the 25th anniversary) is the cleanest version. Use this to test 'Mid-Bass Punch' and vocal centering in your Honda Accord.

songs.title: From the Bottom of My Broken Heart
songs.notes: The ultimate test for 'Early-Vocal Texture'. Unlike the heavily processed later hits, this features a more 'natural' vocal capture. Look for the 2023 Digital Remaster—it preserves the soft piano transients and the 'soulful' moderate tempo (96 BPM). Essential for testing the iFi's ability to render vocal quivers and emotional nuance.

songs.title: Toxic
songs.notes: The ultimate test for 'Soundstage Complexity'. Features high-pitched surf guitar, Bollywood strings, and multi-layered synths. The iFi Velvet signature keeps the high-frequency 'shrieks' from sounding harsh. A genuine hi-res file reveals the distinct 'Air' around the string section.

songs.title: Everytime
songs.notes: The 'Vocal Intimacy' test. A minimalist, piano-driven ballad. Her voice is very 'breathy' here, testing for lower-midrange chestiness. High-resolution versions show a very low noise floor, allowing the delicate piano decay to bloom in your Nonthaburi room.

songs.title: Slumber Party (feat. Tinashe)
songs.notes: Modern 'Spatial Imaging' reference. Features wide-panned percussion and deep sub-bass. The 24-bit/44.1kHz master is essential for testing 'Sub-to-Mid Integration' in the Isuzu MU-X—the bass should be physical but stay perfectly musical.

songs.title: Radio (MTV Unplugged Version)
songs.notes: Mixed by Bob Clearmountain. A masterclass in acoustic soundstage. The tin whistle should have a 'sweet' airiness, not a piercing one. Use the 24-bit/48kHz master from the DVD-A or the 2023 Hi-Res reissue to test the iFi's ability to render complex orchestral pop with a 'black' background.

songs.title: Toss the Feathers (MTV Unplugged)
songs.notes: The ultimate 'Transient Attack' test. Features a high-speed duel between the violin and tin whistle. Tests for 'Slew Rate'—the instruments should stay distinct and textured during rapid-fire passages. A genuine hi-res file reveals the physical 'rasp' of the violin bow.

songs.title: Runaway (1995 Original)
songs.notes: Reference for 'Vocal Intimacy' and midrange bloom. Andrea's voice is very 'breathy' here; tests for lower-midrange chestiness. Use the 24-bit/96kHz Qobuz/Tidal master. The iFi Velvet signature prevents the strings from sounding artificial or 'digital'.

songs.title: Summer Sunshine
songs.notes: Modern Pop-Rock reference. Features a brighter, more polished sound. Use this to test for 'Sibilance' control—if Andrea's 'S' sounds are piercing, your tweeter EQ in the MU-X is too high. The iFi DAC should make this track energetic but fatigue-free.

songs.title: Gimme Shelter
songs.notes: The ultimate test for 'Soundstage Chaos'. Merry Clayton’s vocals should sound physically separate from Mick’s, with a raw, cracking texture at 03:00. The iFi Velvet signature prevents the sharp guitars from becoming fatiguing. Target: 2002 DSD master.

songs.title: Wild Horses
songs.notes: The benchmark for 'Acoustic Layering'. Features multiple acoustic and slide guitars. The SHM-SACD (Super High Material) version offers a cleaner noise floor, allowing you to hear the individual string pluck transients. Essential for testing 'Air' in your Nonthaburi listening room.

songs.title: Sympathy for the Devil
songs.notes: The 'Rhythmic Timing' test. The complex percussion (congas/shakers) must stay perfectly timed with the driving bassline. In your MU-X, use this to test 'Mid-Bass Punch'—if the congas sound 'smeared' into the bass, your car's DSP needs a 0.05ms adjustment.

songs.title: Angie
songs.album: Goats Head Soup (2020 Giles Martin Mix)
songs.notes: Modern hi-res reference. The Atmos-sourced 24-bit/96kHz stereo downmix is incredibly transparent. Features a lush orchestral arrangement that highlights the iFi's lower-midrange warmth. Tests for 'Vocal Intimacy' and string decay.

songs.title: Feeling Good
songs.notes: The ultimate 'Brass Stress Test'. The opening horn blast should sound physical and metallic. Tests the iFi's 'Velvet' smoothing—the horns should be powerful but never 'piercing'. Use the 2005 original CD or the 2024 Hi-Res remaster for the highest dynamic range (DR11).

songs.title: Home
songs.notes: Reference for 'Vocal Intimacy'. Features a very close-mic vocal and a rich acoustic guitar. The iFi Burr-Brown chip will excel at rendering the 'texture' of his voice. In your MU-X, his voice should be pinned to the center of the dash with holographic depth.

songs.title: Cry Me a River
songs.notes: Cinematic Soundstage Test. Features a massive, James Bond-style orchestral arrangement. The transition from the quiet intro to the explosive finale evaluates your amplifier's 'Headroom'. A genuine 24-bit file will show extreme detail in the orchestral decay.

songs.title: Moondance
songs.notes: Reference for 'Pacing and Timing'. The walking bassline and crisp percussion test your car's DSP time alignment. If the bass feels 'behind' the vocals, adjust your millisecond delay. Look for the 20th Anniversary Edition for the cleanest digital transfer.

songs.title: Strong Enough
songs.notes: The benchmark for 'Acoustic Transparency'. Features a minimalist arrangement of acoustic guitar and subtle percussion. The 30th Anniversary remaster (2023) is surprisingly dynamic. Tests for 'Vocal Phasing'—her voice should be laser-centered. If it sounds 'blurry,' check your DSP time alignment.

songs.title: My Favorite Mistake
songs.notes: Reference for 'Electric Guitar Texture'. Features a raw, Wurlitzer-heavy production with deep, organic bass. The original 1998 master (DR11) is superior to the 'Greatest Hits' versions. Evaluates the iFi's ability to render the 'growl' of the lower-midrange without sounding muddy.

songs.title: Wildflower
songs.notes: The 'Orchestral Bloom' test. A departure from her rootsy sound, featuring lush, cinematic string arrangements. Perfect for testing the 'Velvet' signature—the strings should sound deep and rich. Use the 'Acoustic Version' from the Deluxe DVD rip for the ultimate transparency test.

songs.title: I Shall Believe
songs.notes: The ultimate test for 'System Noise Floor'. Starts with a near-silent organ and vocal. The slow build-up requires a high bit-depth to maintain the 'Black Background'. Her vocal sighs and breaths should be clearly audible through the iFi DAC.

songs.title: The One That You Love
songs.album: The One That You Love (MFSL LP/SACD)
songs.notes: The MFSL Reference. Mastered by Mobile Fidelity in 1983 (MFSL 1-113). Features an incredibly clean, wide soundstage. Tests for 'High-Tenor Purity'—Russell’s voice should stay creamy and smooth even on the highest notes. A genuine hi-res file will show harmonic energy reaching past 24kHz.

songs.title: Making Love Out of Nothing at All
songs.notes: Produced by Jim Steinman. A 'Power-Ballad' stress test. Features massive, layered production similar to Meat Loaf. Tests the iFi's 'Congestion Control'—you should still hear the separation between the heavy piano, electric guitars, and the wall of backing vocals during the finale.

songs.title: Goodbye
songs.notes: Produced by David Foster. The ultimate 'Foster Sound' benchmark. Features a deep, polished bassline and crystalline piano. Use this to test 'Sub-to-Mid Integration' in your Isuzu MU-X—the kick drum should be tight and physical, perfectly supporting the airy vocals.

songs.title: Emotions
songs.notes: The ultimate 'High-Frequency Sweetness' test. Her whistle notes at the end of the track reach into the 'Air' region of your spectrogram (above 15kHz). The iFi Velvet signature ensures these notes sound musical and crystalline rather than piercing. Look for the MC30 reissue for the best dynamic range.

songs.title: Vision of Love
songs.notes: Produced by Rhett Lawrence. Benchmark for 90s 'Vocal Presence'. Features a heavy, dry kick drum and intricate melismatic vocals. Tests the iFi's ability to keep the powerful midrange from sounding 'boxy'. Avoid the Greatest Hits versions; they are often too compressed.

songs.title: If It's Over (Live)
songs.notes: Co-written with Carole King. Reference for 'Brass Texture' and live acoustics. The horn section should sound physical and bold, while her live vocals test for 'Soundstage Depth'. Excellent for verifying your Isuzu MU-X's center-imaging.

songs.title: Always Be My Baby
songs.notes: Modern R&B reference for 'Bass Integration'. The rhythmic low-end is punchy but must stay distinct from the layered background vocals. Use this to test the crossover timing in your Honda Accord.

songs.title: Fever
songs.notes: The Holy Grail of vocal imaging. Just Elvis, a double bass, and finger-snaps. Recorded at RCA Studio B. The finger-snaps test 'Transient Attack,' and the bass should have zero boominess. Use the DCC Gold Disc (Steve Hoffman) or the 24-bit/96kHz remaster for the most 'in-the-room' experience.

songs.title: Reconsider Baby
songs.notes: The Blues Reference. Recorded in one magnificent live take. Features a legendary sax solo by Boots Randolph. Tests for 'Dynamic Impact' and 'Instrumental Separation'—the sax should sound physically large and textured. The iFi Burr-Brown chip captures the 'honk' of the sax without making it harsh.

songs.title: Such a Night
songs.notes: The Dual Drummer Test. Features two drummers (Buddy Harman & D.J. Fontana) playing simultaneously. Use this to test 'Rhythmic Timing' and 'Low-End Control' in your Isuzu MU-X—the two kick drums should sound distinct and tight, not a single 'muddy' thump.

songs.title: Thrill of Your Love
songs.notes: Reference for 'Piano Resonance' and 'Vocal Bloom'. Features Floyd Cramer on piano. The iFi Velvet signature excels here, rendering the high piano notes with a 'crystalline' sweetness. Evaluates the soundstage depth of your Nonthaburi listening room.

songs.title: Like a Baby
songs.notes: The 'Vocal Growl' test. Elvis uses a gritty, lower-register delivery. Tests your system's ability to render 'Micro-Dynamics'—you should hear the subtle moisture in his voice. A genuine 24-bit file will show energy reaching well past 30kHz on the harmonica.

songs.title: Love Me Tender (Stereo Master)
songs.notes: Stereo version from the 20th Century Fox stage recordings. Distinguishable by the count-in and the wider soundstage compared to the mono hit.

songs.title: Heartbreak Hotel
songs.notes: The 'Echo Chamber' Benchmark. Recorded in Nashville with a very distinctive, dark reverb. Tests for 'Low-Level Detail'—on your iFi DAC, you should hear the 'decay' of the room clearly. If it sounds 'dry,' your file is likely a low-quality MP3.

songs.title: All Shook Up
songs.notes: Reference for 'Percussive Rhythm'. Features the famous 'slapping' of the back of Elvis's guitar. Tests for 'Transient Attack'—the slaps should be sharp and instantaneous. Great for verifying the speed of your MU-X mid-bass drivers.

songs.title: Love Me Tender
songs.notes: The iconic 1956 Mono mix. High-midrange focus. Tests the 'Chest Resonance' of your center image. If using the iFi ZEN DAC V2, the GTO Filter will help smooth the 8kHz vocal sibilance.

songs.title: Treat Me Nice
songs.notes: The 'Piano Attack' test. Recorded at Radio Recorders, Hollywood. Features a sharp, driving piano. Tests 'Transient Speed'—the hammers hitting the strings should sound crisp and metallic, not blurred.

songs.title: Anyway You Want Me (That's How I Will Be)
songs.notes: The 'Deep Vibrato' benchmark. Recorded at RCA New York. Focuses on Elvis's lower register. Tests 'Mid-Bass Texture' (150Hz-300Hz). In your MU-X, the bass-baritone should feel physically weighty.

songs.title: That's When Your Heartaches Begin
songs.notes: The 'Spoken Word' clarity test. Features a famous mid-song monologue. Tests 'Naturalness'—his speaking voice should sound like he is in the room. If it sounds 'boxy,' your 400Hz DSP gain is too high.

songs.title: Jailhouse Rock
songs.notes: The 'Snare Snap' benchmark. Features a massive, explosive drum sound. The iFi Burr-Brown chip handles the 'aggressive' upper-mids of the vocal performance without making it sound 'shouty'. Look for the 2023 24-bit/96kHz remaster for the cleanest snare decay.

songs.title: Don't Be Cruel
songs.notes: Reference for 'Slap-back Echo'. The echo was created using a dedicated tape machine. In your MU-X, this should feel like the voice is 'expanding' forward from the windshield. If the echo sounds muddy, your DSP 2kHz-4kHz crossover is too wide.

songs.title: Anyway You Want Me (That's How I Will Be)
songs.notes: The 'Deep Baritone' test. Elvis uses his lower register here. The iFi 'Velvet' signature excels at rendering the vibration in his chest. Perfect for testing 'Mid-Bass Texture' (150Hz-300Hz) in your Honda Accord.

songs.title: In the Ghetto
songs.notes: Reference for 'Atmospheric Layering'. Recorded at American Sound Studio. Features a very dry vocal with a slow-building arrangement of strings and backing vocals. The iFi DAC will highlight the grit in his voice and the subtle 'thump' of the snare drum. Target: Vic Anesini 2009 or 2024 Hi-Res master.

songs.title: Long Black Limousine
songs.notes: The 'Emotional Peak' test. Features a massive, building arrangement. The iFi Velvet signature excels here—his voice starts as a whisper and grows to a roar. Tests for 'Dynamic Headroom'—on the MFSL master, the climax should not sound congested.

songs.title: Any Day Now
songs.notes: Burt Bacharach cover. Features a complex, driving bassline and sharp horn stabs. The MFSL version provides a 'blacker' background for the backing vocals to emerge from. In your MU-X, use this to test 'Mid-Bass Integration'.

songs.title: After Loving You
songs.notes: The 'Acoustic Honesty' test. Features an intimate, dry production with prominent acoustic guitar and piano. The iFi Burr-Brown chip renders the 'woody' texture of the instruments beautifully. Target: 2.8MHz DSD stream via Ropieee.

songs.title: Wearin' That Loved On Look
songs.notes: Reference for 'Pacing and Timing'. Features the funky, rhythmic soul of the Memphis Boys. The snare 'snap' and the Hammond organ swirl test for 'Transient Speed'. The MFSL master avoids the 'digital glare' found on 90s CDs.

songs.title: Suspicious Minds
songs.notes: The 1969 Masterpiece. Features the famous 'fake fade-out' at 03:30. This is the ultimate test for 'Phase Coherence' and 'Soundstage Depth'. The MFSL DSD layer provides the cleanest rendering of the walking bassline and the brass stabs. In your MU-X, the backing vocals should feel like a 'halo' around Elvis's centered voice.

songs.title: Blueberry Hill
songs.album: Elvis: That's The Way It Is
songs.notes: Live-in-studio test for 'Vocal Resonance'. In your Isuzu MU-X, his deep baritone hums and sighs should feel physical but stay perfectly centered. If his voice 'blurs' into the piano, your DSP time alignment is slightly off.

songs.title: Can't Help Falling in Love
songs.notes: The Apex of Vocal Balladry. Based on the 18th-century French melody 'Plaisir d'amour'. Features a sensitive, hymn-like delivery. The MoFi SACD or the FTD 'Classic Album' versions are the targets here. Tests for 'Vocal Phasing' and 'Lush Orchestration'—the backing vocals and celeste should sound like they are floating in 3D space.

songs.title: Blue Hawaii
songs.notes: The Soundstage Benchmark. Features a very wide stereo image with steel guitar and backing vocals panned far left/right. The iFi Velvet signature excels at rendering the 'shimmer' of the steel guitar. If the instruments feel 'stuck' to your speakers in Nonthaburi, your toe-in angle needs adjustment.

songs.title: Hawaiian Wedding Song
songs.notes: Reference for 'Duet Imaging' and 'Vocal Purity'. Features beautiful interplay between Elvis and the backing vocalists. Tests for 'Phasing'—the voices should sound layered and holographic, creating a deep 3D arc behind your speakers.

songs.title: Ku-Ui-Po
songs.notes: The 'Rhythmic Texture' test. Features delicate percussion and soft rhythmic guitar. The Burr-Brown chip will prevent the percussion from sounding 'clicky' or artificial. Use this to verify the 'Natural' filter of your iFi DAC—the instruments should sound woody and organic.

songs.title: Moonlight Swim
songs.notes: Reference for 'Mid-Bass Integration'. Features a bouncing, melodic bassline that supports the light arrangement. In your MU-X, the bass should feel 'pillowy' and round, never masking the subtle high-frequency details of the ukulele.

songs.title: Burning Love
songs.notes: The definitive test for 'Rhythmic Drive'. Without the brass overdubs, the interplay between James Burton's guitar and Ronnie Tutt's drums is holographic. Tests the iFi's timing—the cowbell and snare should be sharp and physical. Target: 24-bit/96kHz Hi-Res master.

songs.title: Always On My Mind (Take 2)
songs.notes: The 'Vocal Nuance' benchmark. This raw outtake features Elvis's voice at its most vulnerable, isolated from the lush 'pop' arrangement. The iFi Burr-Brown chip will reveal the slight tremble in his lower register. Perfect for testing 'Midrange Warmth' in your Nonthaburi listening room.

songs.title: T-R-O-U-B-L-E
songs.notes: Reference for 'Vocal Articulation'. A high-speed rockabilly-style track. Elvis's wordplay must stay perfectly distinct. The iFi DAC prevents the fast-paced consonants from sounding 'sheary' or sibilant. Evaluates the 'Slew Rate' of your system.

songs.title: Bird on a Wire
songs.notes: The 'Hotel California' of vocal recordings. Mastered by Bernie Grundman. Features a massive, dry kick drum and complex shakers. Tests the iFi's ability to render 'Vocal Height'—Jennifer's voice should sound like it is floating above the speakers.

songs.title: Fast Car
songs.notes: The benchmark for 'Mid-Bass Punch' and 'Vocal Honesty'. Unlike modern pop, this has zero vocal tuning. The iFi Velvet signature will highlight the raw, 'woody' quality of her voice. Target: Original 1988 CD or 2015 High-Res.

songs.title: Sultans of Swing
songs.notes: The 'Clean Guitar' legend. Mark Knopfler’s Stratocaster is panned with razor-sharp precision. Tests for 'Transient Attack'—the guitar plucks should sound like they are 'sparkling' in the air. The iFi DAC keeps the high-end from sounding like 'glass'.

songs.title: The Moon is a Harsh Mistress
songs.notes: Often cited as the best-sounding vocal album ever recorded. An early digital recording (1982) that sounds incredibly analog. Use this to test for 'Micro-Detail'—you can hear the physical hammers of the piano hitting the strings.

songs.title: You've Got a Friend
songs.notes: The 1971 Original. A masterpiece of 'Organic' production. Tests for 'Analog Texture' and vocal honesty. Look for the Mobile Fidelity (MoFi) or 2024 Hi-Res remaster to hear the true depth of the piano and Carole's soulful, unpolished vocal.

songs.title: You've Got a Friend
songs.notes: The Audiophile Standard. Recorded with extreme clarity and a 'black background'. Features a lush, acoustic arrangement that highlights the 'Velvet' signature of the iFi DAC. Perfect for testing vocal centering and high-frequency 'sweetness'.

songs.title: You've Got a Friend
songs.notes: Jazz-inflected cover. Stacey's 'girlish' and crystalline voice is a high-frequency test for your tweeters. The iFi Burr-Brown chip ensures her voice remains smooth and never becomes 'piercing'.

songs.title: Vincent
songs.notes: Contemporary Jazz-Pop. Joanna's deep, 'lazy' vocal style is a perfect test for the iFi's lower-midrange body. Her version is incredibly intimate and dry, highlighting the 'Velvet' signature of the Burr-Brown chip.

songs.title: Vincent
songs.notes: Premium Audiophile Reference. Features extreme transparency. The acoustic guitar strings have a 'metallic snap' that tests for transient response and jitter. Target: 24-bit/96kHz.

songs.title: Vincent
songs.notes: The Analog Benchmark. Recorded on tape. Features 'natural' warmth and a higher noise floor. Use this to verify your validator's ability to distinguish between 'Vintage Analog' and 'Digital Perfection'.

songs.title: Vincent
songs.notes: Modern Smooth Jazz arrangement. Features a lush, 'velvety' vocal texture with professional orchestral backing. Perfect for testing 'Vocal Liquidity' and midrange warmth. The 2016 digital master is high-resolution, highlighting the iFi ZEN DAC V2's organic Burr-Brown sound.

songs.title: Can't Take My Eyes Off You
songs.notes: The Gold Standard for vocal testing. Features a very slow, bossa-nova style tempo. Tests for 'Breathiness' and 'Vocal Placement'. The iFi Burr-Brown chip will make her voice sound three-dimensional.

songs.title: I Can't Take My Eyes Off You
songs.album: What's Going On: The Audiophile Collection
songs.notes: Soul-Jazz reference. Features a rich piano trio backing. Recorded with extreme clarity to showcase 'Instrumental Texture' and 'Piano Decay'. Target: 24-bit/88.2kHz or 96kHz FLAC.

songs.title: Can't Take My Eyes Off You (Acoustic)
songs.notes: A surprisingly technical acoustic cover. Features a wide soundstage with clean acoustic guitar and powerful vocal dynamics. Use this to test 'High-Frequency Energy' during the chorus.

songs.title: When You Say Nothing At All
songs.album: Now That I've Found You: A Collection
songs.notes: The Bluegrass Reference. Essential for testing 'Air' and high-frequency detail. Her voice is crystalline, which can sound 'thin' on clinical DACs but remains sweet and airy on the iFi Burr-Brown chip.

songs.title: When You Say Nothing At All
songs.notes: The Asian Audiophile Standard. Recorded live-in-studio at 24-bit/96kHz. Features an incredibly intimate, close-mic vocal. Best for testing 'Vocal Centering' and micro-dynamics in a quiet room in Nonthaburi.

songs.title: When You Say Nothing At All (Acoustic Version)
songs.album: When You Say Nothing At All (Single)
songs.notes: The Pop-Acoustic test. Focuses on 'Lower-Midrange' warmth. Ronan's husky baritone is a great test for your car's DSP—if his voice sounds 'boomy,' you need to lower the 200Hz-400Hz range.

songs.title: My Girl
songs.notes: Instrumental piano & cello cover. Reference for 'String Resonance'. The cello's low notes test your car's subwoofer crossover, while the high piano notes test for DAC jitter. Pure Resonance: The Instrumental Series.

songs.title: My Girl (feat. Casey Abrams)
songs.notes: Recorded Live at Sunset Sound. A 'Funk' take with high energy. Features Casey Abrams on vocals. Great for testing 'Transient Attack' of the drums and Wurlitzer. High dynamic range (DR11+).

songs.title: My Girl
songs.notes: Pure acoustic guitar and vocal. Very clean, modern production. Use this to verify 'Sibilance' control on your iFi DAC—the close-mic recording is very revealing of sharp 's' sounds.

songs.title: My Girl
songs.notes: The 1964 original. Use the 24-bit/96kHz Qobuz/Tidal master. Tests for 'Analog Tape Hiss' and 60s stereo panning. Note: Lead vocals are often panned hard right or left in early stereo mixes.

songs.title: My Girl
songs.notes: Legendary audiophile benchmark. Features a minimalist arrangement of just voice, bass, and finger-snaps. The finger-snaps are a 'Transient Speed' test, while the bass line tests your system's 'Pitch Definition'. Target: 2011 Mobile Fidelity (MoFi) or Impex 1STEP remaster.

songs.title: What a Wonderful World
songs.notes: Posthumous charity duet. Combines Eva's 1996 live vocals with Katie's 2007 studio production. Tests for 'Vocal Phasing' and the iFi's ability to distinguish between Eva's raw, analog-sourced voice and Katie's polished digital studio capture.

songs.title: I Only Want to Be with You
songs.notes: Audiophile favorite. Recorded on 2-inch analog tape at Capitol Studios. Features a stunning, minimalist arrangement that tests for 'Instrumental Separation' and 'Midrange Depth'. The iFi Burr-Brown chip will excel at rendering the warmth of the bass and the silkiness of her vocals.

songs.title: I Don't Wanna Talk About It
songs.notes: Scottish audiophile vocal reference. Features a blend of Celtic acoustic instruments and traditional pop production. Known for its 'Ethereal' vocal texture. The iFi Velvet signature is essential to prevent the violin and acoustic guitar from sounding 'glassy'.

songs.title: I Knew I Loved You
songs.notes: Scottish audiophile cover. Replaces the 90s synths with a smooth saxophone and lush acoustic arrangement. Perfect for testing 'Midrange Warmth' and the iFi's ability to render smooth saxophone overtones without harshness.

songs.title: Have You Ever Seen the Rain
songs.notes: Chesky Records Binaural+ recording. Captured using a 'Binaural Head' microphone in a church in Brooklyn. Features a stunning upright bass and raw, growling vocals. Essential for testing '3D Soundstage' and 'Instrument Placement'. Target: 24-bit/192kHz MQA or FLAC.

songs.title: Against All Odds (Take a Look at Me Now)
songs.notes: Recorded during the 1990 Seriously, Live! tour. Features a more robust vocal delivery than the 1984 studio original. The 2016/2019 remasters are 'louder' but the original 1990 CD master retains superior dynamic range (DR13). Pure Resonance: The Stadium Series.

songs.title: Always on My Mind
songs.notes: Produced by Lars Martin Myhre. A masterclass in 'Admirably Restraint'. Features a spacious, airy soundstage with deep double-bass anchoring the track. Essential for testing 'Breathiness' and vocal micro-dynamics on the iFi Burr-Brown chip. Pure Resonance: The Audiophile Standard.

songs.title: Under the Bridge (2014 Remastered)
songs.notes: Produced by Rick Rubin. Reference for 'Raw' guitar texture and dry vocal imaging. The ending choir (Gail Frusciante and friends) tests for soundstage depth and height. Avoid lossy iTunes versions for critical listening. Target: Original 1991 or 2020/2024 BG cuts.

songs.title: Stairway to Heaven
songs.notes: The holy grail of dynamic progression. Moves from a 73 BPM acoustic folk intro to a 100 BPM hard rock finale. Tests for 'Black Background' during the recorder intro and 'Transient Speed' during the Page solo. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: Smells Like Teen Spirit
songs.notes: The ultimate test for 'Macro-dynamics'. The jump from the quiet verse to the explosive chorus will reveal if your iFi DAC handles transients with 'Velvet' smoothness or 'Digital Glare'. Target: 1991 Original or 1996 MFSL master.

songs.title: Ain't No Mountain High Enough
songs.notes: Produced by Ashford & Simpson. The spoken-word intro tests for 'Vocal Presence' and dry recording textures. The massive orchestral swell in the finale is a torture test for DAC headroom and instrument separation. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: Ain't No Sunshine
songs.notes: Chill-Out Jazz arrangement. Tests for 'Rhythmic Swing' and acoustic instrument placement. Her voice is breathier than Dana Winner's, which tests the iFi's ability to render fine vocal micro-details.

songs.title: Upside Down
songs.notes: Produced by Nile Rodgers & Bernard Edwards (Chic). A masterclass in transient speed. The 'sharp' guitar scratching and aggressive snare drum hits test the 'Velvet' smoothing of the iFi chip. Look for harmonics reaching 24kHz+.

songs.title: Touch Me in the Morning
songs.notes: Reference for 'Silk' vocal texture. The 2012/2021 remasters provide a cleaner noise floor to evaluate the iFi's ability to render micro-reverb decay. High-frequency 'air' should be prominent in the final fade-out.

songs.title: Love Hangover
songs.notes: The ultimate test for 'Pacing and Timing'. Starts as a sultry ballad and shifts into a high-energy disco groove. Tests the iFi's ability to maintain rhythmic tight-bass during the transition. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: I'm Coming Out
songs.notes: Reference for 'Trombone and Brass' texture. The opening blast should sound metallic and physical. Modern 24-bit remasters (2021) show energy up to 24kHz. High-transparency test for the Isuzu MU-X DSP setup.

songs.title: Theme from Mahogany (Do You Know Where You're Going To)
songs.notes: Benchmark for 'String Lushness'. Evaluates the Velvet signature's ability to render a wide orchestral arc without sounding 'mushy'. High-resolution versions reveal the subtle analog tape hiss of the original 1975 master.

songs.title: Endless Love (with Lionel Richie)
songs.notes: The definitive 'Duet Imaging' test. You should hear Lionel on the right and Diana on the left with a clear phantom-center where their voices blend. Tests for 'Phase Coherence' in your Honda Accord.

songs.title: Linger
songs.album: Everybody Else Is Doing It, So Why Can't We?
songs.notes: Produced by Stephen Street. The benchmark for 'Vocal Air' and string-section lushness. The 1993 original master has a massive DR13 rating. Tests the iFi's ability to render the delicate breathiness of Dolores' voice. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: Zombie
songs.notes: Reference for 'Heavy Transient' response. Moves from a clean, melodic bassline to aggressive, distorted guitar walls. Tests for 'Congestion'—you should still hear the separation of instruments during the loud chorus.

songs.title: Ode to My Family
songs.notes: Elite test for 'Imaging'. The 'Doo-doo-doo' vocal intro should sound holographic and centered. The 2020/2025 remasters offer 24-bit/96kHz clarity but watch your validator for compression compared to the original CD.

songs.title: Dreams
songs.album: Everybody Else Is Doing It, So Why Can't We?
songs.notes: The gold standard for 'Jangle-Pop' layering. Features complex vocal harmonies and 12-string guitar textures. The 2018/2025 remasters are great, but the original CD has superior dynamic range. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: When You're Gone
songs.notes: A soul-infused rock ballad. Tests for 'Beefy' production and drum-kick impact. Her vocal delivery here is one of her most powerful. A genuine 24-bit file will reveal the distinct separation between the punchy rhythm section and the jangly guitars.

songs.title: Animal Instinct
songs.notes: Reference for 'Acoustic Clarity'. The opening guitar riff should sound metallic and physical. Tests the 'Velvet' signature's ability to keep the high frequencies smooth without losing the 'string-pluck' detail.

songs.title: Promises
songs.notes: The band at their heaviest. Extreme test for 'Distortion Control'. Evaluates if your system can render thick walls of electric guitar without losing the focus on Dolores' centered vocal.

songs.title: Daffodil Lament
songs.notes: An 6-minute epic that moves through multiple movements. Perfect for testing 'Soundstage Transition'. The slow-burning intro requires a very low noise floor (high bit-depth) to hear the subtle room acoustics.

songs.title: All Over Now
songs.notes: From their final studio album. Features modern digital production mixed with her original demo vocals. Tests for 'Posthumous Seamlessness'. A high-res 24-bit/96kHz file will show energy all the way to 48kHz.

songs.title: Fly Me to the Moon
songs.notes: Classic audiophile vocal reference. Features an incredibly intimate 'Close-Mic' recording. Tests for 'Sibilance' control and the natural decay of the acoustic guitar. The iFi Velvet signature should make this sound like honey.

songs.title: Sometimes When We Touch
songs.notes: Reference for 'Vocal Texture'. Her breath control and mouth sounds are clearly audible. Requires a high bit-depth to maintain the 'Black Background' during the quietest passages. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: Driving
songs.notes: Excellent for 'Soundstage Width'. Features a light, airy arrangement with wide-panned percussion. Tests the channel separation of your Honda Accord or Isuzu MU-X DSP setup.

songs.title: L-O-V-E
songs.notes: Jazz-pop arrangement with bright brass and crisp drums. Tests for 'High-Frequency Energy' (HFE). A genuine 24-bit master will show harmonic 'air' reaching well above 22kHz on your spectrogram.

songs.title: You and Me
songs.notes: Modern Mandopop production with higher compression than her early Jazz work. Use this to test your '16-bit vs 24-bit' logic—many Mandopop masters are 16-bit upscaled to 24-bit containers.

songs.title: You're Beautiful
songs.notes: Produced by Tom Rothrock. Essential test for high-frequency acoustic guitar transients and vocal 'rasp'. Modern digital recording with high-frequency energy extending to 24kHz+. Evaluates the 'Velvet' smoothing of the iFi Burr-Brown chip.

songs.title: Goodbye My Lover
songs.notes: Piano-driven reference for midrange transparency. Tests for 'digital glare' in the upper-mids. A genuine 24-bit master should show a very low noise floor (-110dB+) due to modern silent recording environments.
Retro-style production with a modern wide soundstage. Excellent for testing 'Phase Coherence' and the separation of the layered backing vocals and Wurlitzer piano.

songs.title: Goodbye My Lover
songs.notes: Piano-driven reference for midrange transparency. Tests for 'digital glare' in the upper-mids. A genuine 24-bit master should show a very low noise floor (-110dB+) due to modern silent recording environments.

songs.title: Thinking Out Loud
songs.notes: Classic test for vocal clarity and 'dry' snare drum hits. The 24-bit/44.1kHz or 96kHz masters are essential to avoid 'sizzling' high frequencies on the iFi Burr-Brown chip. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: Perfect
songs.notes: Modern power-ballad reference. Evaluates the layering of orchestral strings against a centered male vocal. Excellent for checking 'Soundstage Width' in your Honda Accord or Isuzu MU-X setup.

songs.title: The A Team
songs.notes: Minimalist production reference. Tests for 'intimacy' and acoustic guitar string-pluck detail. A genuine 24-bit file should show a very high dynamic range (low compression).

songs.title: Close to You
songs.notes: Japanese Audiophile Master. High-transparency vocal recording. Essential for testing 'Sibilance' (the 's' and 't' sounds). The iFi Burr-Brown chip should make her voice sound intimate and rounded, never piercing. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: Moon River
songs.notes: Minimalist jazz arrangement. Features deep acoustic bass and delicate piano. Tests for 'Black Background'—there should be zero digital hiss between her vocal phrases. Genuine 24-bit Hi-Res is the target version.

songs.title: 500 Miles
songs.notes: Tests for midrange 'texture'. Her breathing and the 'wetness' of the vocal recording should be clearly audible. Evaluates the soundstage depth and instrument separation of the iFi ZEN DAC.

songs.title: Everybody Plays the Fool
songs.notes: Produced by George Massenburg and Linda Ronstadt. A modern audiophile masterpiece. Tests for 'Sibilance' and vocal texture. The percussion should 'snap' cleanly while his voice remains airy and centered. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: Tell It Like It Is
songs.notes: 1966 Soul classic. Tests for 'Vintage Transparency'. The 2021 remasters (or the Analogue Productions vinyl rip) show amazing depth. The mono-to-stereo imaging transition is a great test for your car DSP.

songs.title: Louisiana 1927
songs.notes: Reference for 'Dynamic Range'. Moves from a whisper to a massive orchestral swell. Tests if the iFi Burr-Brown chip can maintain its 'Velvet' smoothness during loud, complex passages.

songs.title: Just the Way You Are
songs.notes: Produced by Phil Ramone. Benchmark for Fender Rhodes piano texture and center-imaged saxophone breathiness. High-resolution remasters should show 18-20 bits of real depth. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: Scenes from an Italian Restaurant
songs.notes: Dynamic range torture test. Evaluates amplifier slew rate and the system's ability to pivot from a solo piano ballad to an upbeat Dixieland jazz ensemble without congestion.

songs.title: What's New
songs.notes: Audiophile gold standard for orchestral soundstage depth. The strings should feel holographic and positioned far behind the centered vocal. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: New York State of Mind
songs.notes: Reference for grand piano resonance and room decay. The 2011/2021 remasters provide the most transparent view of the original 1976 analog master tapes.

songs.title: Long Long Time
songs.notes: Masterclass in 70s folk-rock vocal texture. Evaluates the 'woodiness' of the acoustic guitar and the emotional transparency of the lower-midrange frequencies.

songs.title: Chan Chan (2021 Remaster)
songs.album: Buena Vista Social Club (25th Anniversary Edition)
songs.notes: 2021 High-Res transfer from original 1/2-inch tapes. The benchmark for 'Velvet' organic texture. Tests for the 'air' around the trumpet and the 'woody' resonance of the double bass. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: Blue Bayou
songs.notes: Reference for vocal dynamics and 'Velvet' midrange. The 2015 Remaster (24/192) is the target version. Spectrogram should show organic roll-off above 20kHz with no brick-walling.

songs.title: Please Forgive Me
songs.notes: Produced by 'Mutt' Lange. Elite test for vocal center-imaging and 'Velvet' midrange bloom. The late drum entry tests system headroom and the transition from intimate acoustic textures to massive power-ballad dynamics. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: Stay (Live in Prague)
songs.notes: Reference for pipe-organ sub-bass and dynamic headroom. Tests the iFi's ability to drive complex, multi-layered cinematic textures without congestion.

songs.title: Raiders March (The Berlin Concert)
songs.notes: Modern hi-res reference for brass texture and soundstage width. Orchestration should bloom far beyond the physical boundaries of the speakers.

songs.title: Giorgio by Moroder
songs.notes: The ultimate test for phase coherence and system timing. Features an evolution from intimate lounge jazz to massive electronic-orchestral fusion.

songs.title: The Planets: Mars, the Bringer of War
songs.notes: Orchestral stress test for transient speed and layering. Requires high-speed amplification to maintain rhythmic precision during heavy brass sections.

songs.title: You Want It Darker
songs.notes: Elite test for basso profondo and micro-detail. The iFi Burr-Brown chip should render the 'labored air' of the vocal without grain or boominess.

songs.title: Hey Laura
songs.notes: Reference for 'velvet-wrapped-gravel' vocal presence. Evaluates midrange warmth and the natural bloom of piano and saxophone layering.

songs.title: Down In The Hole
songs.notes: Cavernous blues reference for sub-bass control and 'spooky' realism. Tests for vocal depth and low-end authority in a dark soundstage.

songs.title: Colour to the Moon
songs.notes: Stockfisch Records reference. Elite test for acoustic precision and phase coherence. Pinpoint imaging of guitars requires a jitter-free Ropieee stream.

songs.title: Estate
songs.notes: The ultimate benchmark for orchestral bloom and vocal decay. Requires a low noise floor to capture the silence between the slow piano notes. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: East of the Sun (and West of the Moon) [Live]
songs.notes: Reference for soundstage depth and live ambience. Evaluates the ability to separate the piano, upright bass, and audience noises in a holographic 3D space.

songs.title: Beautiful Love
songs.notes: Features Miles Davis. Tests phase coherence and instrument positioning. The warm, muted trumpet should remain distinct from the lush vocal texture.

songs.title: L-O-V-E
songs.notes: Modern hi-res reference for vocal intimacy. Tests for micro-dynamics and 'wet' vocal texture. Requires a balanced midrange to avoid clinical coldness.

songs.title: Blue Train
songs.notes: Reference for instrument separation and horn layering. Weckl's snare transients and Weckl/Patitucci rhythm section test system speed.

songs.title: Seven Steps to Heaven
songs.notes: High-speed technical stress test. Evaluates bass definition and the iFi's ability to maintain analog warmth in a fast-moving, dense brass mix.

songs.title: The Jazz Police
songs.notes: Modern high-energy reference. Tests dynamic headroom and amplifier stress during massive, simultaneous horn section 'stabs'.

songs.title: Count Bubba
songs.notes: Reference for phase coherence and soundstage width. Rhythmic shuffle requires perfectly timed interaction between mid-bass and high-frequency percussion.

songs.title: Suite No. 1 in G Major, BWV 1007: I. Prelude
songs.notes: The gold standard for cello resonance. Tests resonant depth and bowing texture. Cello should sound physically large and centered. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: Mediterranean Sundance
songs.artist: Al Di Meola & Paco de Lucía
songs.notes: Reference for transient speed and tonal accuracy. Must maintain clear distinction between nylon-string and steel-string guitar textures.

songs.title: Cello Suite No. 6 in D Major, BWV 1012: I. Prelude
songs.notes: Modern hi-res reference for room decay and ambience. Evaluates dynamic range and the system's ability to render a wide, atmospheric soundstage.

songs.title: Marcel
songs.notes: Elite test for low-level detail and piano attack. Mechanical noises (pedals, finger slides) provide the 'truth' of the performance. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: Cantate Domino
songs.notes: The ultimate test for soundstage height and pipe organ depth. The '2-mic' recording technique provides the most natural spatial information possible.

songs.title: Lux Aurumque
songs.notes: Reference for harmonic 'shimmer' and cluster-chord resolution. Requires high phase-coherence to prevent the complex harmonies from sounding gritty.

songs.title: Pie Jesu (Requiem)
songs.notes: A Keith O. Johnson (Reference Recordings) masterpiece. Features 3D vocal imaging and extreme low-frequency organ pedal testing.

songs.title: O Magnum Mysterium
songs.notes: Elite test for ensemble separation and vocal purity. The eight voices must remain distinct within a holographic semi-circle soundstage.

songs.title: Keith Don't Go (Live)
songs.notes: The benchmark for guitar transients and string resonance. Tests the 'Burr-Brown' warmth of the iFi DAC against sharp acoustic attacks.

songs.title: Birds
songs.notes: Reference for vocal layering and spatial height. The sub-bass plucks test the iFi's low-end texture and control. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: Exactly Like This
songs.notes: Reference Recordings high-res masterpiece. Evaluates uncompressed dynamic range and the 'air' of a real recording space.

songs.title: Celestial Echo
songs.notes: Electronic soul reference for jitter testing and holographic imaging. Synths should move seamlessly through a 3D soundstage.

songs.title: I’m Confessin’ (That I Love You)
songs.notes: The holy grail of acoustic space; listen for background club ambiance and 3D depth. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: You Look Good to Me
songs.notes: Bowed bass and delicate piano; tests for low-frequency resonance and harmonic accuracy. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: I Don’t Care Anymore

songs.title: If Leaving Me Is Easy (2015 Remaster)

songs.title: Take Me Home

songs.title: Long Long Way to Go

songs.title: Inside Out
songs.notes: Tests midrange separation during the high-energy outro; listen for the distinct placement of the sax and lead guitar.

songs.title: Don't Lose My Number
songs.notes: Classic 80s transient test. Electronic tom-tom rolls should be sharp and punchy with no overhang.

songs.title: We Said Hello Goodbye

songs.title: I Wish It Would Rain Down
songs.notes: Features Eric Clapton; a masterpiece of 'Velvet' blues. Tests midrange bloom and the ability to resolve a full gospel choir. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: Another Day in Paradise
songs.notes: Reference for vocal layering and centered kick drum. David Crosby's backing vocals should be clear and distinct. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: All of My Life
songs.notes: Introspective jazz-pop. Elite test for micro-dynamics, vocal presence, and delicate brass textures.

songs.title: Something Happened on the Way to Heaven
songs.notes: Dynamic stress test. The horn section should have a massive, clean impact without sounding harsh.

songs.title: In the Air Tonight
songs.notes: The definitive live drum break. Elite test for dynamic impact, sub-bass speed, and huge soundstage atmosphere.

songs.title: One More Night
songs.notes: A 'Velvet' masterpiece. Replaces digital drums with organic percussion; tests for realistic saxophone texture and vocal warmth. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: Separate Lives
songs.notes: Reference for vocal imaging. The two voices should be perfectly placed and distinct within the stereo field.

songs.title: Take Me Home
songs.notes: An 8-minute stress test for system headroom and layering. The climax should be massive but never muddy.

songs.title: In The Air Tonight (2015 Remastered)
songs.notes: The ultimate test for gated reverb and dynamic impact. The drum entry at 3:41 evaluates amplifier headroom and mid-bass punch. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: I Don't Care Anymore (2016 Remastered)

songs.notes: Reference for dual-layered percussion and soundstage width. Tests midrange resolution and door panel resonance control.

songs.title: Against All Odds (2016 Remastered)
songs.notes: A 'Velvet' reference for piano resonance and vocal micro-dynamics. Requires a low system noise floor to capture the intimate intro. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: Against All Odds (Take a Look at Me Now)
songs.notes: Produced by Arif Mardin. The ultimate test for 'Vocal Intimacy' vs. 'Orchestral Power'. The piano intro requires a silent noise floor, while the final chorus tests for midrange congestion. Look for the 2016 'Take a Look at Me Now' remasters.

songs.title: Can't Turn Back the Years (2016 Remastered)
songs.notes: Elite test for sub-bass control and 'Velvet' smoothness. The sustained low-end requires perfect subwoofer-to-mid integration.

songs.title: The Look

songs.title: It Must Have Been Love

songs.title: Spending My Time

songs.title: Listen to Your Heart

songs.title: Fading Like a Flower (Every Time You Leave)

songs.title: (Do You Get) Excited?

songs.title: Crash! Boom! Bang!

songs.title: Vulnerable

songs.title: I Never Loved a Man (The Way I Love You) [MTV Unplugged]
songs.notes: Soulful power-vocal test. Refines high-volume midrange stability and dynamic range. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: (Everything I Do) I Do It For You

songs.title: Us and Them

songs.title: Shine On You Crazy Diamond (Pts. 1-5)
songs.notes: Elite test for transparency and tonal accuracy. The guitar vibrato and layered analogue synths demand high-level resolution. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.notes: Stockfisch reference for vocal texture. Tests lower-midrange 'woodiness' and chest-voice resonance. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.notes: The ultimate test for deep, sustained bass definition and acoustic guitar clarity. Sub-bass must be controlled, not boomy. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: No Sanctuary Here

songs.title: I Can't Stand the Rain
songs.notes: Reference for vocal texture and high-frequency air. Tests tweeter naturalness and center-imaging stability. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: Closer
songs.notes: Tests mid-bass integration and the 'roundness' of acoustic percussion. The kick drum should feel physical but stay perfectly musical.

songs.title: The Same Kind Words
songs.notes: A 'Velvet' masterclass in micro-detail. Finger-slide sounds and guitar harmonics must be crisp and lifelike.

songs.title: Still Loving You
songs.notes: Reference for vocal dynamics. Use the original 1984 mix to preserve the full dynamic range. The guitar 'bloom' tests soundstage width. Pure Resonance: The Jazz 200.

songs.title: Wind of Change
songs.notes: The whistle intro is an elite test for tweeter naturalness. Perfectly centered drums and vocals make it ideal for fine-tuning DSP time alignment.

songs.title: Wind of Change (MTV Unplugged)
songs.notes: Reference for 'Vocal Duet Imaging'. The blend between Klaus Meine and Morten Harket tests for midrange separation. Features an 'army of acoustic guitars' and a full string section. Pure Resonance: The Live Series.

songs.title: Send Me An Angel
songs.notes: Complex layering of synths and acoustic guitars. Tests for soundstage depth and the ability to resolve atmospheric textures without congestion.

songs.title: In Trance
songs.notes: Raw 70s production. Features Uli Jon Roth’s organic guitar tone; tests midrange accuracy and the physical 'grit' of electric blues-rock.

songs.title: What’s Going On
